title: What is DeepSeek? The low-cost AI model, explained
lede: DeepSeek is an AI company known for powerful, low-cost, openly available models — including strong reasoning models that rival pricier rivals. Here's what it is, what it's good at, and what to weigh before using it.
keyTakeaways: DeepSeek is an AI company (based in China) known for capable, cost-efficient models, including strong reasoning and coding models.
Many of its models are openly available, and it's free to try via the DeepSeek app and website.
It's best for budget-conscious users who want strong reasoning and coding without premium pricing.
Consider data and privacy: some organizations restrict tools from certain providers, so check your workplace's policy before using it for sensitive work.
description: What is DeepSeek? A plain-English explainer on the low-cost AI model — what it is, what it's good at, how it compares to ChatGPT, and what to consider before using it.

sections.heading: What is DeepSeek?
sections.html: DeepSeek is an AI company, based in China, known for building powerful AI models that cost far less than many rivals — and for releasing several of them openly. Its models, including well-regarded reasoning and coding models, made headlines for matching pricier competitors at a fraction of the cost. You can use DeepSeek free through its app and website, much like ChatGPT, and developers can run several of its open models themselves.

sections.heading: What is DeepSeek good at?
sections.html:
• Reasoning — its reasoning models work through multi-step problems well.
  • Coding — a frequent strength in its model line.
  • Cost efficiency — strong performance at low or no cost, which is its headline appeal.
  • Openness — several models are openly available for developers to run and build on.

sections.heading: How to use DeepSeek
sections.html: For most people, using DeepSeek looks just like any other AI assistant: open the app or site, type a request in plain language, and refine the answer. The same prompting skills apply — give context, a role, a clear task, and the format you want. See how to write a good AI prompt. And as with any model, it can be confidently wrong, so verify important claims.

sections.heading: What to consider before using DeepSeek
sections.html: One practical note: DeepSeek is built by a China-based company, and some employers, schools, and governments restrict tools based on where data is processed. Before using it for sensitive or work data, check your organization's policy — the same caution applies to any AI tool. For general learning and personal use, it's a capable, low-cost option. Weighing trade-offs like this is part of AI literacy.

sections.heading: DeepSeek vs ChatGPT: which should you use?
sections.html: ChatGPT is the more polished all-rounder with the biggest ecosystem; DeepSeek's draw is strong reasoning and coding at low cost. For most everyday users, ChatGPT is the easier default; DeepSeek appeals to the budget-conscious and to developers who want open models. The full breakdown is in ChatGPT vs DeepSeek, and the wider field is in the best AI apps. Whatever you use, the skill of directing it — AI fluency — matters most; check yours with the free AI IQ test.

faq.q: What is DeepSeek in simple terms?
faq.a: DeepSeek is an AI company, based in China, known for powerful, low-cost AI models — including strong reasoning and coding models — several of which are openly available. You can use it free via its app and website, similar to ChatGPT.
Yes, you can use DeepSeek for free through its app and website, and several of its models are openly available for developers. Some advanced or high-volume usage may have paid options.

It depends on your priorities. DeepSeek is known for strong reasoning and coding at low cost; ChatGPT is the more polished all-rounder with a larger ecosystem. For everyday use, ChatGPT is the easier default; DeepSeek appeals to budget-conscious users and developers.

For general learning and personal use it's a capable tool, but because it's built by a China-based company, some organizations restrict it over data-processing concerns. Check your workplace or school policy before using it for sensitive data — advice that applies to any AI tool.
